message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_pthread_mutex (bd-dvr22 priority-7 subset)

## FULL REVIEW MODE verification

Two workspace test runs this pass:
- Run 1: glibc_internal_abi_test SIGABRTs with `free(): invalid size`
  — bd-ye0f4 regressed for the 3rd iteration after e289f838's TLS
  dtor test capture fix. Already filed (bd-ye0f4 is now re-closed
  per the close_reason, but the abort keeps coming back under default
  parallelism).
- Run 2: pthread_abi_test SIGABRTs at test cancel_running_thread
  (still bd-qhnxc, open, last-session filing).

No NEW HIGH/CRITICAL beads to file — the two recurring aborts are
each already tracked. clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
remains clean.

## bd-dvr22 priority-7 (partial)

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_mutex.rs covers
the pthread mutex + mutexattr subset of the pthread surface (~18
symbols out of the module's 141):

- pthread_mutex_init / _destroy / _lock / _trylock / _unlock
- pthread_mutex_timedlock (with 1 µs absolute deadline — contended
  mutex returns ETIMEDOUT cleanly, uncontended succeeds)
- pthread_mutex_consistent
- pthread_mutexattr_init / _destroy
- pthread_mutexattr_{settype,gettype,setprotocol,getprotocol,
  setpshared,getpshared,setrobust,getrobust}

Oracles:
- Return-code contract: every call returns 0 or a documented
  non-zero errno (via `rc >= 0`, since pthread errno is always
  non-negative).
- Attribute round-trip: set→get recovers the same value for type,
  protocol, pshared, robust.
- Lifecycle invariants: destroy on an unlocked mutex succeeds;
  destroy-then-reuse paths surfaced through MarkStale ops; the
  harness tracks per-mutex State (Live / LockedBySelf / Stale) and
  depth for recursive mutexes.
- NORMAL mutex double-lock-by-same-thread is UB — skipped
  deliberately; RECURSIVE mutex successive locks tracked by depth.
- Global PTLOCK serializes iterations; cleanup() drains any held
  locks before destroy.

Single-threaded per iteration; a separate TSan campaign is still
the right way to catch real mutex races and is tracked as the
natural follow-up under bd-dvr22's 'pthread (TSan)' priority. This
harness catches the large class of non-race bugs (init/destroy
mis-ordering, invalid type rejection, attribute round-trip, stale-
handle ops) that are valuable independently of TSan.

Clean cargo build (2.4s) + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings
(after two clippy auto-lint fixes: `rc == 0 || rc > 0` → `rc >= 0`
for pthread error-only signed returns, and one `needless_lifetimes`
elision on pick_slot).

Continues bd-dvr22: mmap → signal → socket → fortify → setjmp →
dlfcn → pthread-mutex (subset). Remaining: pthread cond/rwlock/
barrier/key harnesses, c11threads (TSan).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,6 +90,11 @@ name = "fuzz_dlfcn"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_dlfcn.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_pthread_mutex"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_mutex.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
